sglang.title: Improve moe reduce sum kernel performance
sglang.author: kkHuang-amd
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
torch.sum could not use GPU core efficiency, implement specific kernel to enhance the performance
Modifications
change the base docker image and modify torch.sum to ops.moe_sum in fused_moe.py

sglang.title: Speed up `update_weights_from_tensor`
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Speed up by avoiding tensor serialization and deserialization. Since it was only used for tests before and never released, the old one was OK, and the new one with API breaking changes seems OK.
Modifications

sglang.title: Eagle speculative decoding part 3: small modifications to the general scheduler
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
This is used for speculative decoding in #2150. It includes all small modifications to the general scheduler.
Co-authored-by: yukavio <kavioyu@gmail.com>

sglang.title: Eagle speculative decoding part 4: Add EAGLE2 worker
sglang.author: yukavio
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Support eagle speculative decoding. The following results are obtained on a single H100.
Official eagle code: 200 token/s
see https://github.com/SafeAILab/EAGLE
Normal decoding speed (SGLang): 156 token/s
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf

sglang.title: [Feature, Hardware] Enable DeepseekV3 on AMD GPUs
sglang.author: BruceXcluding
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Support DeepseekV3 on AMD Instinct MI300X GPU


Modifications

Add proper fix for AMD FP8 e4m3fnuz to support DeepseekV3 FP8 model
Bypass FlashInfer backend bmm_fp8 to cast FP8 to BF16 in MLA
Add AMD triton stages config

TODO

 amd base image testing #2692
 sgl-kernel add amd backend #3

sglang.title: Fix package loss for small models
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Fix for #2548
fix packet loss when deploy small llm like qwen 0.5B or fishauido 

## Motivation

We found that when deploying some small LLMs, SGlang experiences packet loss under high load because the scheduler's results return too quickly, and at the same time, the rid is not updated in rid_to_sta

vllm.title: [VLM][Bugfix] Multi-modal processor compatible with V1 multi-input
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes an issue where inputs with multiple multi-modal items fail on V1 for models that use the merged multi-modal processor.
Also, renamed MultiModalFieldItem to MultiModalFieldElem and introduced a wrapper class MultiModalKwargsItem to avoid confusion with MultiModalDataItems.

vllm.title: [mypy] Pass type checking in vllm/inputs
vllm.author: CloseChoice
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Supports #3680 (link existing issues this PR will resolve)
I ignored the assert_never lines since this seems like an open mypy issue:

python/mypy#14833

vllm.title: [VLM] Merged multi-modal processor for LLaVA-NeXT
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR implements the merged multi-modal processor for LLaVA-NeXT. To avoid redundant code, I have introduced base classes VisionEncoderInfo and BaseLlavaMultiModalProcessor.

vllm.title: According to vllm.EngineArgs, the name should be distributed_executor_backend
vllm.author: chunyang-wen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix the wrong argument name in doc.
in vllm.EngineArgs, the argument should be distributed_executor_backend instead of distributed-executor-backend.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Free cross attention block table for preempted-for-recompute sequence group.
vllm.author: kathyyu-google
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes the error "AssertionError: block table already exists" for encoder-decoder models, when the scheduler attempts to reschedule a sequence that was previously preempted for recomputation.
The error occurs at the following location: 
  
    
      vllm/vllm/core/block_manager.py

vllm.title: [V1][Minor] Optimize token_ids_cpu copy
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently, we don't consider the actual lengths in copying rows of token_ids_cpu. This small PR optimizes it by tracking the actual lengths.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Change kv scaling factor by param json on nvidia gpu
vllm.author: bjmsong
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
According to the docs, when the checkpoint is in FP16, the kv-cache scaling factors can be loaded via --quantization-param-path.
With the help of this change, the accuracy will increase.




kv_cache_dtype
quantization_param_path
gsm8k




Before
fp8
No
0.22



fp8
Yes
0.22


After
fp8
No
0.22



fp

vllm.title: [Misc] Minimum requirements for SageMaker compatibility
vllm.author: nathan-az
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #11557
Implements /ping and /invocations, and creates an alternate dockerfile, identical to vllm-openai but with entrypoint setting port to 8080.
Since the OpenAI server is more "production-ready" we use this functionality and its handlers as the base.
Considerations:
Dockerfile
The Dockerfile

as per PR #10544
FIX #11319

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Check chain_speculative_sampling before calling it
vllm.author: houseroad
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The error is reported by type checker, and it's legit.
Since we can have the chain_speculative_sampling as None meanwhile user still set use_flashinfer=True.

vllm.author: khluu
